paper_replicated.citation: Oliveira et al., Tactical Asset Allocation with Macroeconomic Regime Detection

summary: Headline numbers in README come from four independent milestone runs executed between 2026-03-13 and 2026-03-23. This manifest documents which script, data coverage, and seed policy produced each reported figure. See README 'Reproducibility Notes' for known sources of drift across reruns.

headline_runs.purpose: Extended FRED coverage run that produces the IS/OOS Sharpe headline numbers.
headline_runs.seed_policy: KMeans(random_state=42). Ridge in rolling walk-forward uses alpha=1.0 deterministically.
headline_runs.notes: Not reproduced by 'python regime_pipeline.py' alone; run 'python gap_closing.py' to regenerate.

headline_runs.purpose: Per-ETF and per-regime Information Coefficient decomposition.
headline_runs.seed_policy: Inherits KMeans(random_state=42) from regime_pipeline. No explicit seed for IC computation itself (Spearman rank is deterministic given inputs).
headline_runs.notes: IC magnitudes are sensitive to FRED data revisions because full-sample K-means centroids (and therefore historical regime labels) shift when input data changes.

headline_runs.purpose: Cross-sectional permutation test: shuffle regime labels across time and re-run the ridge forecasting step to build a null distribution of strategy Sharpe.
headline_runs.seed_policy: N_PERMUTATIONS=200. BASE_SEED=20260323. Each permutation i uses np.random.RandomState(BASE_SEED + i), making the distribution fully reproducible given the same input data.
headline_runs.notes: p = 0.055 is borderline at the conventional 0.05 threshold. This p-value is DIFFERENT from the pipeline-internal permutation test in regime_pipeline.py (which is unseeded and legacy). The README's reported p comes from THIS script.

headline_runs.purpose: Per-ETF RMSFE vs. Random Walk and AR(1) baselines, both in-sample and out-of-sample.

key_outputs.interpretation: Essentially indistinguishable from random walk on point forecasts; edge is in cross-sectional ranking.

headline_runs.notes: Confirms the decomposition claim: ridge beats RW on ranking but not on absolute magnitude.

known_non_determinism.description: Internal permutation test uses np.random.permutation(orig_labels) without a fixed seed. Empirical p-value from this path will drift across reruns.
known_non_determinism.status: legacy / exploratory path; NOT the source of the README's p ≈ 0.055. That figure is from negative_control.py which is seeded.
known_non_determinism.recommended_fix: Fix a seed (e.g. BASE_SEED=20260323 to match negative_control.py) or remove this path.

known_non_determinism.description: Historical values are subject to revision. FRED series availability can change over time (currently ~8 series fail in the baseline pipeline).
known_non_determinism.status: external factor, not a code bug. Documented in README 'Reproducibility Notes'.
known_non_determinism.recommended_fix: None possible in code; users who need a fixed data snapshot can pin FRED vintage data externally.

fred.baseline_coverage: 76 series typically successful; 8 series currently unavailable (x-suffix exchange rates: EXSZUSx, EXJPUSx, EXUSUKx, EXCAUSx; plus OILPRICEx, AMDMNOx, ANDENOx, CONSPI)
fred.gap_closing_coverage: Gap-closing run maps some unavailable tickers to alternatives (e.g. OILPRICEx -> MCOILWTICO, EX*x -> DEX*) and reaches 104 transformed variables.

nber.purpose: Recession dating for stress-test and Type I/II analysis.
